subject: Accounting
title: IFRS vs. GAAP: The Global Debate
description: A micro-lesson that makes IFRS vs. GAAP: The Global Debate usable.

hook.text: Two companies, identical operations, different standards—different reported profits.

buildup.text: IFRS is principles-based: broad guidelines, more judgment. GAAP is rules-based: detailed specifics.

discovery.text: Revenue recognition, lease accounting, and inventory methods all differ between the two.

twist.text: Some companies report under both standards simultaneously—double the work, double the cost.

climax.text: Full convergence was once the dream, but political and practical barriers keep it out of reach.

punchline.text: Same business, different rules, different numbers.

quiz.question: How does IFRS differ from GAAP?
options: IFRS is principles-based while GAAP is rules-based
GAAP applies only outside the U.S.
